data.title: SoundHound AI upgraded following 'transformative acquisition': Cantor Fitzgerald
data.description:
SoundHound AI was upgraded to Overweight from Neutral by Cantor Fitzgerald as its acquisition of Amelia, an enterprise artificial intelligence software company, is expected to almost double its revenue in 2025.

SoundHound, which specializes in conversational AI solutions for businesses, announced the acquisition on Thursday, along with their second quarter 2024 financial results, which beat estimates.

With 2024 revenue expected to hit at least $80M, SoundHound now projects 2025 revenue to exceed $150M due to the acquisition. Amelia is slated to add $45M through recurring AI software revenue along with professional services and agent-related fees.

SoundHound shares surged 21% by the time markets closed Thursday. They were down slightly during pre-market trading on Friday.

Cantor Fitzgerald increased its price target to $7 from $5.

"We believe this could be a transformative acquisition for SOUN," said Cantor Ftizgeral analyst Brett Knoblauch, in a note.

"Amelia will add financial services and healthcare customer exposure to SOUN, and while they would have entered these markets, it likely would have taken them several years to do so," he added.

SoundHound acquired Amelia for $10M in cash, $70M in stock, and agreed to pay down $70M of Amelia's $100M debt.

"In other words, a purchase multiple of ~4.5x for a company that has meaningful recurring revenue and is operating across very synergistic end markets," Knoblauch said.

This follows the June acquisition announcement of Allset, a food ordering platform with nearly 7,000 restaurant partners in the U.S.

SoundHound revealed locking down a number of new deals with major clients. Its voice AI ordering system will be rolled out at Beef 'O' Bradys' in more than 20 states across the US. It also signed deals with a major pizza chain to handle phone ordering services. What's more, Employee Assist, SoundHound’s voice AI offering for back-of-house staff, will be utilized by two coffee shop chains.

SoundHound expanded its partnership with Stellantis (STLA) to six brands.

SoundHound has a Hold rating from Seeking Alpha analysts, but a Buy rating from Wall Street analysts. It has a Hold rating from Seeking Alpha's Quant system, which routinely beats the market.

data.title: LANXESS Aktiengesellschaft reports Q2 results; initiates Q3 and reaffirms FY24 outlook
data.description:
• LANXESS Aktiengesellschaft press release (OTCPK:LNXSF): Q2 EBITDA pre exceptionals up 69.2 percent at €181 million.

  • Revenue of €1.68B (-5.6% Y/Y).

  • LANXESS is confirming its forecast for the current fiscal year 2024 and continues to anticipate an increase in earnings of 10 to 20 percent compared to the previous year’s figure of €512 million EBITDA pre exceptionals. For the third quarter of 2024, LANXESS expects EBITDA pre exceptionals to be close to or up to the level of the second quarter. For the fourth quarter, the Group then anticipates a more muted performance as a result of the normal seasonal development.

data.title: Algonquin Power & Utilities dividend declines by 40.1% to $0.065
data.description:
• Algonquin Power & Utilities (NYSE:AQN) declares $0.065/share quarterly dividend, -40.1% decrease from prior dividend of $0.108.
  • Forward yield 4.2%
  • Payable Oct. 15; for shareholders of record Sept. 30; ex-div Sept. 30.
  • See AQN Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Sendas Distribuidora reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Sendas Distribuidora (NYSE:ASAI): Q2 EBITDA in the quarter totaled R$ 965 million, an increase of R$ 150 million or +18.4%, with a margin of 5.4% (+0.3 p.p. vs. 2Q23).
  • Revenue of R$17.87B (+11.8% Y/Y).
  • Net income in the pre-IFRS16 view totaled R$ 165 million in 2Q24, with net margin of 0.9%.
  • The operational cash generation totaled R$ 7.6 billion over the past 24 months.
